透

TŌSHI
The Seer · lift the veil

Open Tōshi on any WebCenter page and she reveals what it's built from - every dashboard, saved search and task, nested as it really is on the page, each one a click from its editor. On a task page a pinned TASK row names its task type and links straight to it. Stop hunting the system to find what's in front of you.

◉ in-browser, instant ↗ click straight to the editor ▦ X-ray the whole page ◎ point & locate ⤓ export a handover doc ⌨ drive it from the keyboard

実Live - Tōshi at work synthetic data · no customer info

The real extension running on a sample WebCenter project. Everything here is made up - generic names, a demo company - so this is exactly the tool, on nobody's data.

透 Pinpoint
Stop hunting. Hover a script in the panel and its field or tab glows on the page.

壱What it is & why it exists

A WebCenter page is a stack of nested dashboards, embedded saved searches and task pages - and nothing on screen tells you which is which or where to edit them. The old reflex was to dig through the dashboard manager and guess. Tōshi reads the page you're already on and lays the structure bare.

見 Sees what's in front of you

Scoped to the page you opened it on - the dashboards/searches/task actually rendered, nested by their real DOM structure. Not a whole-instance crawl, just this page.

↗ Every name is a doorway

Each dashboard and search name links straight to its editor (dashboard.jsp?…&edit=1, the saved-search editor, …). See it, click it, edit it.

{} Sees the scripts too

One toggle and every dashboard script on the page shows up nested under its dashboard - display and run scripts, each with its Dashboard Context (Project / Document / Task / My Work) and type (visibility, on change, validation, …). The logic behind the glass, not just the layout. On a page thick with scripts, filter by context and type: the chips start greyed, click one to light it up and the list narrows to just those scripts. Hover a script row and it lights up its exact target: a bound field, or its layout block - so a tab-visibility script highlights just that tab, not the whole page.

⧉ Every window, at once

Open it in two browser windows, ten tabs, whatever - they never collide. Tōshi injects fresh on the page you're looking at and keeps no shared state, so there is nothing to clear, ever. Inspect one project in one tab and another beside it - each reads its own page, with no stale overlay carried over and nothing to reset between them.

弐Install (load unpacked)

Tōshi is a lean Chrome extension - our own code, no build step. It injects only when you click its icon (activeTab + scripting, no host permissions).

  1. Open chrome://extensions
  2. Turn on Developer mode (top-right toggle)
  3. Click Load unpacked and select the extension folder
    …\kage-gumi\extensions\toshi
  4. Pin 透 Tōshi to the toolbar (puzzle-piece icon → pin)
  5. Open a WebCenter page and click the 透 icon - the panel slides in. Click the icon again to toggle it off.

Not on the Chrome Web Store. Tōshi is an unlisted extension - it isn't publicly searchable or listed anywhere. The only way to get it is a direct link from me. If you don't have that link, ask me for it - there's no public download.

参Browser compatibility

Tōshi is a Manifest V3 extension built on Chromium APIs - chrome.scripting with a MAIN-world probe and a shadow-DOM panel. The line is simple: any Chromium browser that loads an unpacked MV3 extension runs her; Gecko and WebKit would need a port.

BrowserEngineStatusNotes
Google Chrome Chromium · Blink ✓ full Primary target - every build is tested here. Load unpacked from chrome://extensions.
Microsoft Edge Chromium · Blink ✓ full Same MV3 + chrome.scripting + MAIN-world probe. Load unpacked from edge://extensions. Often the sanctioned Chromium browser on a locked-down corporate laptop.
Brave Chromium · Blink ✓ full Identical engine and APIs. Load unpacked from brave://extensions.
Opera · Vivaldi · Arc Chromium · Blink ◐ works Chromium siblings - load-unpacked behaves the same. Not part of the tested set, but no reason she wouldn't run.
Firefox Gecko ✕ not as-is MV3 differs - the browser.* namespace, a non-service_worker background, and newer/limited MAIN-world injection. Would need a port.
Safari WebKit ✕ no Requires converting to a Safari Web Extension via Xcode - no load-unpacked. Not on the roadmap.

Rule of thumb. If the browser has an …://extensions page with a Developer mode toggle and Load unpacked, Tōshi runs - that's every Chromium browser. Where Chrome is locked down on a corporate machine, that usually means Edge.

肆The toolbar - every control

The panel header. Each button is a single, focused action. Active toggles glow blue.

⤷
Reference graph ⤷ refs
Switch from the on-page view to the deep LOADDASHBOARD graph - every dashboard each one links to (sub / button / task / approval + embedded searches), with DELETED flags.
◎
Pick & locate
Click any element on the page → Tōshi finds it, scrolls to its row, and marks it. Both the row and the element keep a persistent blue glow until you pick another.
▦
Reveal all ▦ all
Outline every element on the page at once (each name a clickable link to its editor) and stash the panel - the whole page X-rayed, a launchpad. Labels nudge apart so nested ones never hide each other.
⤓
Export
Copy the whole structure to your clipboard as a Markdown outline - ticket / handover ready. Shift-click for JSON.
#
Toggle IDs
Show / hide the [bracketed] ids. Off by default - you rarely need them while scanning. Filtering still matches ids even when hidden.
{}
Dashboard scripts
Show the scripts attached to each dashboard - display and run scripts (with their type: visibility, on change, validation, …), read from the dashboard's config and nested under it. A counter at the top tallies how many were found, and a (N) badge flags any script attached more than once. Hover a script → its place on the page highlights in violet. Like the dashboard builder's Behavior panel, a script bound to one attribute (marked ⊙ field) lights up that precise field; display and section scripts keep the whole-section highlight as a fallback. Full-page sections are outlined (see-through), not washed, so the page stays readable.
⇄
Dock side
Flip the panel to the other edge when it's covering something you need to see.
-
Collapse
Slide Tōshi off to a thin 透 grip tab so the whole page is clear. Click the tab to bring her back.
↻
Re-scan
Re-read the page (after it changes, or you navigate within it).
⌕
Type-to-filter filter…
Narrow a busy tree by name or id. The matched text is highlighted on the hits; ancestor rows stay for context.
⧉
Copy row
Hover a row → ⧉ copies its name [id].
⌨
Keyboard shortcuts ?
Every control here has a single-key shortcut while the panel is focused - P pick, A reveal all, / filter, E export (Shift for JSON), Esc cancel/close, and more. Press ? for the cheat-sheet. Toggle Tōshi itself from anywhere with Alt+Shift+T - rebindable at chrome://extensions/shortcuts. Single letters stay scoped to the panel, so they never clash with WebCenter or the browser.

透 Which instance, always

A slim bar under the header reads the live WebCenter version and host of the instance you're on - so you always know which environment you're inspecting. Read once, from your own session.

 WebCenter 26.03 · acme-dev.esko-saas.com

⌕ Filter that shows its work

Type and the matched text lights up on the actual hits - ancestors stay dim for context, so you see exactly what matched.

dashStartPage
dashLHD_CreateProject
dashLHD_ProjectCreationHeader

◎ Pick & locate - both ways

Click an element on the page; its row is marked in the list and the element keeps a blue glow on the page. One selection, persistent.

dashProject_Details
Project_Details… the element, lit on the page

▦ Reveal all - the X-ray

Outline everything at once, panel stashed. Every label is a link (↗) to its editor - the map doubles as a launchpad.

StartPage ↗ 
My Projects ↗ 

▣ Task pages, named by type

On a task page Tōshi shows a single pinned TASK row at the top of the panel: it names the task type and links straight to its task type page, so you know at a glance what task page you're on. The type is resolved via GetProjectTasks (the same call the WCR Inspector used); the task itself isn't outlined on the page. Works on the modern task execution page (wctaskexecutionpage.jsp), where there is no task form - the id is read from the page URL.

taskSTY_ProvideSustainabilityData ↗

⌨ Keyboard-driven

Every action has a key while the panel is focused. Press ? for the cheat-sheet; toggle Tōshi from anywhere with Alt+Shift+T (rebindable). Single letters stay scoped to the panel, so they never clash with WebCenter or the browser.

Pick element P
Reveal all A
Filter /
Export · JSON E Shift+E

陸Two views - don't confuse them

Tōshi shows structure two different ways. Default is what's in front of you; the graph is the deep dependency map.

default

ON PAGE

The dashboards, searches and task page actually rendered on this page, nested by DOM containment - a parent is the nearest ancestor that's also inspectable. No network calls. Scoped to what you can see.

⤷ refs

REFERENCE GRAPH

Walks each on-page dashboard's full LOADDASHBOARD config - every dashboard it links to, recursively (sub / button / task / approval + embedded searches), cycle-safe, with DELETED dashboards flagged. The deep dependency map - opt in when you want it.

漆Good to know

  • It tracks the page. Highlights and the reveal-all outlines re-position as you scroll or resize.
  • Nested labels never hide each other. Reveal-all nudges colliding labels down so all stay readable.
  • The page stays usable. Only the labels are clickable - the outline boxes are click-through, so you can still work the page underneath.
  • Filter by id even with ids hidden. The match looks at the underlying text, not just what's on screen.
  • It reads your live session. The reference graph fetches same-origin, carrying your WebCenter cookie - nothing extra to log into.
  • It names the instance you're on. A bar under the header reads the live WebCenter version + host (from productinfo), so you never mistake which environment you're inspecting. Hidden on a non-WebCenter page.
  • It tells you its own build. A small version stamp sits beside the title in the header (read straight from the extension manifest), so you always know which Tōshi build you're running.
  • It's keyboard-driven. Every toolbar action has a single-key shortcut while the panel is focused (press ? for the cheat-sheet), and the panel toggles from anywhere with Alt+Shift+T - rebindable at chrome://extensions/shortcuts. Single letters stay scoped to the panel, so nothing clashes with WebCenter or the browser.
  • Self-contained & safe. Renders in a shadow DOM (WebCenter's CSS can't leak in, and Tōshi never touches the page's own styles).
  • Task type, in two clicks. On a task page Tōshi reads the task type (via GetProjectTasks) and links both its general-info page and its specifications/execution page. The Default vs Task Execution view is chosen from that page's own Current View dropdown - it switches in place, so there is no separate URL.

捌Roadmap - where the eye is going

Tōshi grows on her own track (透 I-XI), from "sees this page" to "reads any WebCenter and hands you the map nobody wrote down." What's live today, and what's next.

零Born · 27 Feb 2026 — where the eye first opened

壱
Page Inspector透 I
The base: the on-page tree, the ⤷ refs reference graph, every name a link to its editor. The CLI, the KG-web 透 panel, and this extension.
✓ shipped
弐
Highlight & Locate透 II
Devtools-grade: hover to highlight the real block on the page, ◎ pick to locate any element in the tree, type-to-filter, copy, export. The overlay layer.
✓ shipped
参
Full Surface透 III
Live WebCenter version in the header, plus a MAIN-world probe that surfaces attribute categories and names the task type + links its execution page (via GetProjectTasks) - each one click from its editor.
✓ shipped
肆
Linked Surface透 IV
Drill into the references. Every attribute category expands to its document references; each reference to its linked documents, and each document to its own attribute categories - one click from the editor. The deep content map a project's references actually form, surfaced in place.
✓ shipped
伍
Support Bundle透 V
The logs support always asks for, one click away: server.log, SEVERE.log and catalina.log, downloadable as a version-stamped bundle straight from the panel. Pairs with Mei for first-pass triage.
▲ next up
陸
Project History透 VI
On a project page, pull the full audit trail - status changes, document versions, approvals, task actions, member and attribute edits - read straight from the project's history, filterable and exportable as a support/handover artifact. The story of how the project got to now, in the panel.
planned
漆
The Map透 VII
Export the topology to Miro - a customer-facing diagram of how their WebCenter is actually wired, from a node-link graph (shared hubs stand out). A deliverable, not a debug view.
planned
捌
See → Act透 VIII
Hand any node to the crew: "document this" (Haku writes the spec) or "ask about this" (Mei for a support answer). Recon becomes the first step of a build.
planned
玖
Dashboard Atlas透 IX
The whole instance, not one page: the entire dashboard forest, with orphans, dead references and circular refs flagged. An architecture-review artifact for inheriting or migrating a solution.
planned
拾
Time Machine透 X
Snapshot a reference graph, re-run later, diff it - added / removed / newly-deleted. "What changed since the handover or the migration?"
planned
拾壱
Pinpoint透 XI
Highlight precision: a script bound to one attribute lands on that exact field, and a script attached to a layout block lands on that block - so a tab-visibility script highlights just the tab it hides, not the whole dashboard. Found by reading what the script's config names (the attribute, or the block id) and matching it in the rendered DOM, mirroring the builder's Behavior panel. The eye, surgical.
✓ field + block/tab shipped
